text:
It was a quiet afternoon in the old library where I was nestled reading a very interesting book about obscure insects. Life had a lot of ups and downs, but when a particularly fascinating butterfly landed on a peculiar bloom, I couldn't help but admire its vibrant colors.

The butterfly fluttered around the flower, gathering nectar, its abdomen shimmering like liquid moonlight. I watched it lazily sip the sweet nectar, then suddenly I felt something unusual—a warmth spreading through my limbs.

I blinked, staring into the depths of the flower. Was that...? No. Somehow—it felt almost magical—I knew. I reached out a delicate feathered leaf.

The butterfly paused. Did it know something? Its eyes fluttered. It couldn't? Ah! Nectar. I pulled. Nothing.

But... oh, was it?

With wings-outstretched majestically, the butterfly took flight. I smiled and felt full-filled.

For my trouble, one night, I dreamt. It
